_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: iw step-done/step-fail crash with UndefinedColumn when worktree ORM adds columns to step_runs/work_items

steps.description: Replace full ORM select(Model) with column-projected selects (load_only) in orch/cli/step_commands.py and orch/cli/item_commands.py for every agent-facing read of StepRun/WorkItem/WorkflowStep; pin a per-module core column set; add module docstring; append 'CLI resilience to in-flight schema drift' subsection to docs/IW_AI_Core_Agent_Constraints.md

Review S01 — confirm every agent-facing callsite is patched, no daemon files modified, no migration added, projected column set covers every downstream attribute access, docstring + constraints doc updated

Reproduction + regression tests in tests/integration/cli/test_step_commands_drift.py — one scenario per patched CLI command, against a column-drifted testcontainer DB, asserting both exit code AND DB side-effect; manual RED-check verifying suite would have caught the bug pre-fix

Review S03 — coverage of every patched callsite, semantic-correctness assertions (not just shape), real drift simulation (drops columns the ORM still declares), subprocess invocation (not direct call), testcontainer convention compliance, plausible RED-check narrative

Cross-step global review S01..S03 — confirm chain holds end-to-end (every patched callsite has a regression test, every test exercises a patched callsite), all ACs satisfied, no daemon files modified, no migration added
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
